title: Trials Expanded
description: A Minecraft mod that allows trial vaults and spawners to be mined and crafted
body:
# Trials Expanded **A Minecraft mod that allows trial vaults and spawners to be mined and crafted** ![NeoForge](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Hyperbole-Devs/vectors/8494ec1ac495cfb481dc7e458356325510933eb0/assets/cozy/supported/neoforge_vector.svg)
## Description Trials Expanded is a Minecraft mod that allows trial vaults and spawners to be mined and crafted. This mod adds new crafting recipes for (non-ominous) vaults and spawners, as well as a smithing template for upgrading vaults to ominous vaults. Additionally, the vault upgrade smithing template can be found as loot in Vaults and Ominous Vaults with Ominous Vaults having a higher chance to drop it. This vault upgrade smithing template can be copied like any other smithing template. ## Features - Minable (Ominous) Vaults and Trial Spawners with silk touch - Crafting recipes for Vaults and Trial Spawners - Smithing template for upgrading Vaults to Ominous Vaults - Vault Upgrade Smithing Template as Vault loot ## Recipes ### Vault ![Vault Recipe](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/touchtheduck/assets/refs/heads/main/trialsexpanded/vault_crafting_recipe.png) ### Trial Spawner ![Trial Spawner Recipe](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/touchtheduck/assets/refs/heads/main/trialsexpanded/trial_spawner_crafting_recipe.png) ### Vault Upgrade ![Vault Upgrade Recipe](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/touchtheduck/assets/refs/heads/main/trialsexpanded/vault_upgrade_smithing_template_crafting_recipe.png) ### Ominous Vault ![Ominous Vault Recipe](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/touchtheduck/assets/refs/heads/main/trialsexpanded/ominous_vault_smithing_recipe.png) ## Mod Loaders Trials Expanded is currently only available for Neoforge. I'm currently not planning on porting this mod to other mod loaders. ## Backports I'm currently not planning on backporting this mod to earlier Minecraft versions than 1.21.1, but if you want you can try it yourself and open a pull-request on my github repository. ## Credits * [DevIn's Badges](https://intergrav.github.io/devins-badges-docs) for the badges used in this README
